sections.label: Section 1470
sections.original: κοὐδεὶς ὑπέστη , πεδία δʼ ἐξεπίμπλασαν
sections.translation: Not one withstood our onset , and they filled the plain with fugitives , while blood was streaming from the countless dead our spears had slain . When victory had crowned our warfare , some set up an image of Zeus as a trophy , others were stripping the Argive dead of their shields

sections.label: Section 1475
sections.original: σκυλεύματʼ εἴσω τειχέων ἐπέμπομεν .
sections.translation: and sending their spoils inside the battlements ; and others with Antigone are bringing the dead here for their friends to mourn . So for the city , the result of this struggle hovers between the two extremes of good and evil fortune . Exit Messenger .

sections.label: Section 1480
sections.original: οὐκ εἰς ἀκοὰς ἔτι δυστυχία
sections.translation: No longer do the misfortunes of this house extend to hearsay only ; three corpses of the slain lie here at the palace for all to see ; by one common death they have drawn their lot , a life of darkness .

sections.label: Section 1485
sections.original: οὐ προκαλυπτομένα βοτρυχώδεος
sections.translation: I do not veil my tender cheek shaded with curls , nor do I feel shame , from maiden modesty , at the dark red beneath my eyes , the blush upon my face , as I hurry on , in bacchic revelry for the dead ,

sections.label: Section 1490
sections.original: ων , κράδεμνα δικοῦσα κόμας ἀπʼ ἐ-
sections.translation: casting from my hair its mantle and letting my delicate saffron robe fly loose , a tearful escort to the dead . Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1493
sections.original: ὦ Πολύνεικες , ἔφυς ἄρʼ ἐπώνυμος · ὤμοι μοι , Θῆβαι ·
sections.translation: Oh , Polyneices ! you were rightly named , after all ; woe to you , Thebes !

sections.label: Section 1495
sections.original: σὰ δʼ ἔρις — οὐκ ἔρις , ἀλλὰ φόνῳ φόνος —
sections.translation: Your strife — not strife , but murder on murder — has brought the house of Oedipus to ruin with dire and grim bloodshed . What harmonious or tuneful wailing can I summon ,

sections.label: Section 1500
sections.original: δάκρυσι δάκρυσιν , ὦ δόμος , ὦ δόμος ,
sections.translation: for my tears , my tears , oh , my home ! oh , my home ! as I bear these three kindred bodies , my mother and her sons , a welcome sight to the Fury ? She destroyed the house of Oedipus , root and branch ,

sections.label: Section 1505
sections.original: τᾶς ἀγρίας ὅτε
sections.translation: when his shrewdness solved the Sphinx ’ s unsolvable song and killed that savage singer . Alas for you , father ! What other Hellene or barbarian ,

sections.label: Section 1510
sections.original: τῶν προπάροιθʼ εὐγενετᾶν
sections.translation: what mortal from a noble line ever endured the anguish of such visible afflictions ?

sections.label: Section 1514
sections.original: τάλαινʼ , ὡς ἐλελίζει —
sections.translation: Ah ! poor girl , how piteous is your cry !

sections.label: Section 1515
sections.original: τίς ἄρʼ ὄρνις , ἢ δρυὸς ἢ
sections.translation: What bird , perched on the high-leaved branches of oak or pine , will come to mourn with me , left motherless ? With cries of woe ,

sections.label: Section 1520
sections.original: τοῖσδε προκλαίω μονάδʼ αἰ-
sections.translation: I lament before it comes the piteous lonely life , that I shall live for the rest of time , in streaming tears . On which of these

sections.label: Section 1525
sections.original: τας σπαραγμοῖς ἀπαρχὰς βάλω ;
sections.translation: shall I throw my offerings first , plucking the hair from my head ? on the breast of the mother that suckled me , or beside the ghastly death-wounds of my brothers ’ corpses ?

sections.label: Section 1530
sections.original: ὀτοτοτοῖ λεῖπε σοὺς
sections.translation: Oh , oh ! Oedipus , my old father with sightless eyes , leave your house , reveal the misery of your life , you who have cast a mist of darkness over your eyes and

sections.label: Section 1535
sections.original: σοῖσι βαλὼν ἕλκεις μακρόπνουν ζόαν .
sections.translation: draw out a weary existence within the house . Do you hear , you who are wandering with old step across the court , or sleeping on your wretched pallet couch ?

sections.label: Section 1539
sections.original: τί μʼ , ὦ παρθένε , βακτρεύμασι τυφλοῦ
sections.translation: Why , daughter ,

sections.label: Section 1540
sections.original: ποδὸς ἐξάγαγες ἐς φῶς
sections.translation: have you dragged me to the light by your piteous tears , supporting my blind footsteps , from the gloom of my bed-chamber , gray-haired , invisible as a phantom of the air , or as a spirit from the world below , or

sections.label: Section 1545
sections.original: πτανὸν ὄνειρον ;
sections.translation: as a dream that flies ?

sections.label: Section 1546
sections.original: δυστυχὲς ἀγγελίας ἔπος οἴσῃ ,
sections.translation: Father , there are tidings of sorrow for you to bear ; no longer do your sons see the light , or your wife , who would always labor to tend your blind footsteps as with a staff .

sections.label: Section 1550
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ὤμοι .
sections.translation: Alas for you , my father !

sections.label: Section 1551
sections.original: ὤμοι ἐμῶν παθέων · πάρα γὰρ στενάχειν τάδʼ , ἀυτεῖν .
sections.translation: Alas for my sorrows ! I may well groan and cry . Three lives ! Tell me , child , by what fate they left the light .

sections.label: Section 1555
sections.original: οὐκ ἐπʼ ὀνείδεσιν οὐδʼ ἐπιχάρμασιν ,
sections.translation: I do not say this to reproach or mock you , but in sadness : your own avenging curse , with all its load of swords and fire and ruthless war , came on your sons . Alas for you , my father !

sections.label: Section 1560
sections.original: αἰαῖ .
sections.translation: Ah me !

sections.label: Section 1560b
sections.original: τί τάδε καταστένεις ,
sections.translation: Why that groan ?

sections.label: Section 1561
sections.original: τέκνα .
sections.translation: My sons !

sections.label: Section 1561b
sections.original: διʼ ὀδύνας ἔβας ·
sections.translation: You are in pain ; but if you could look towards the sun-god ’ s four-horse chariot and turn the light of your eyes on these corpses —

sections.label: Section 1565
sections.original: τῶν μὲν ἐμῶν τεκέων φανερὸν κακόν ·
sections.translation: The evil fate of my sons is clear ; but she , my poor wife , tell me , daughter , by what fate did she die ?

sections.label: Section 1567
sections.original: δάκρυα γοερὰ
sections.translation: All saw her weep and heard her moan , as she rushed forth to carry to her sons her last appeal , a mother ’ s breast .

sections.label: Section 1570
sections.original: ηὗρε δʼ ἐν Ἠλέκτραισι πύλαις τέκνα
sections.translation: But the mother found her sons at the Electran gate , in a meadow where the lotus blooms , fighting out their duel with spears , like lions in their lair , eager to wound each other ,

sections.label: Section 1575
sections.original: ἤδη ψυχρὰν λοιβὰν φονίαν ,
sections.translation: a murderous libation of blood already cold , owed to Hades , poured out by Ares . Then , taking from the dead a sword of hammered bronze , she plunged it in her flesh , and in sorrow for her sons fell with her arms around them . So the god who fulfills these sorrows has brought them all together on this day ,

sections.label: Section 1580
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ἁμετέροισι δόμοισιν ἄχη θεὸς ὃς
sections.translation: father , for our house .

sections.label: Section 1582
sections.original: πολλῶν κακῶν κατῆρξεν Οἰδίπου δόμοις
sections.translation: Today is the beginning of many troubles to the house of Oedipus ; may he live to be more fortunate !

sections.label: Section 1584
sections.original: οἴκτων μὲν ἤδη λήγεθʼ , ὡς ὥρα τάφου
sections.translation: Cease now your lamentations ; it is time we thought of

sections.label: Section 1585
sections.original: μνήμην τίθεσθαι · τόνδε δʼ , Οἰδίπου , λόγον
sections.translation: their burial . Hear what I have to say , Oedipus . Eteocles , your son , left me to rule this land , by giving it as a dowry to Haemon with his marriage to your daughter Antigone . Therefore I will no longer allow you to dwell in this land ;

sections.label: Section 1590
sections.original: σαφῶς γὰρ εἶπε Τειρεσίας οὐ μή ποτε
sections.translation: for Teiresias clearly said that the city would never prosper as long as you made your home here . So begone ! And I say this not in insult , nor because I am your enemy , but from fear that some calamity will come upon the land , through those avenging fiends of yours .

sections.label: Section 1595
sections.original: ὦ μοῖρʼ , ἀπʼ ἀρχῆς ὥς μʼ ἔφυσας ἄθλιον
sections.translation: O destiny ! From the beginning , how you have created me wretched and unhappy , if any mortal ever was ; for before I had left my mother ’ s womb and seen the light , Apollo foretold to Laius that I , then unborn , should become my father ’ s murderer ; alas for me !

sections.label: Section 1600
sections.original: ἐπεὶ δʼ ἐγενόμην , αὖθις ὁ σπείρας πατὴρ
sections.translation: So , as soon as I was born , the father who begot me tried to kill me , thinking me his enemy , for it was fated he should die at my hand ; so he sent me unweaned to make a pitiful meal for beasts ; I escaped from that — would that Cithaeron

sections.label: Section 1605
sections.original: ἐλθεῖν Κιθαιρὼν εἰς ἄβυσσα χάσματα ,
sections.translation: had sunk into hell ’ s yawning abyss , because it did not destroy me , but . . . Fate made me a slave in the service of Polybus . And I , poor wretch , after slaying my own father came to my mother ’ s bed , to her sorrow ,

sections.label: Section 1610
sections.original: παῖδάς τʼ ἀδελφοὺς ἔτεκον , οὓς ἀπώλεσα ,
sections.translation: and begot sons that were my brothers , whom I have destroyed , by bequeathing to them the legacy of curses I received from Laius . For I was not born so foolish , that I should have contrived these things against my own eyes and my children ’ s life , without some god .

sections.label: Section 1615
sections.original: εἶἑν · τί δράσω δῆθʼ ὁ δυσδαίμων ἐγώ ;
sections.translation: Let that pass . What am I , poor wretch , to do ? Who now will be my guide and tend the blind man ’ s step ? The one who is dead ? If she were alive , I know well that she would . My pair of noble sons ? But they are gone from me . But am I still so young myself that I can find a livelihood ?

sections.label: Section 1620
sections.original: πόθεν ; τί μʼ ἄρδην ὧδʼ ἀποκτείνεις , Κρέον ;
sections.translation: Where ? O Creon , why do you seek in this way to kill me utterly ? For you will kill me , if you banish me from the land . Yet I will never twine my arms about your knees and seem a coward , for I would not betray my former nobility , no ! not for all my ills .

sections.label: Section 1625
sections.original: σοί τʼ εὖ λέλεκται γόνατα μὴ χρῴζειν ἐμά ,
sections.translation: You have spoken well , in refusing to touch my knees , but I could not allow you to dwell in the land . Of these dead , bear one at once to the palace ; but the other , who came with strangers to sack his native town , the dead Polyneices ,

sections.label: Section 1630
sections.original: ἐκβάλετʼ ἄθαπτον τῆσδʼ ὅρων ἔξω χθονός .
sections.translation: cast forth unburied beyond the borders of this land . To all the race of Cadmus shall this be proclaimed : Whoever is caught decking his corpse with wreaths or giving it burial , shall be requited with death . let him be unwept , unburied , a meal for birds .

sections.label: Section 1635
sections.original: σὺ δʼ ἐκλιποῦσα τριπτύχους θρήνους νεκρῶν
sections.translation: As for you , Antigone , leave your mourning for these lifeless three and go indoors , to lead your maiden life until to-morrow , when Haemon waits to marry you .

sections.label: Section 1639
sections.original: ὦ πάτερ , ἐν οἵοις κείμεθʼ ἄθλιοι κακοῖς .
sections.translation: O father , in what cruel misery are we plunged !

sections.label: Section 1640
sections.original: ὥς σε στενάζω τῶν τεθνηκότων πλέον ·
sections.translation: For you I mourn more than for the dead ; for in your woes you do not have something that is grievous and something not ; but you were born wholly unfortunate , father . As for you , new-made king , I ask you , who do you insult my father with banishment ?

sections.label: Section 1645
sections.original: τί θεσμοποιεῖς ἐπὶ ταλαιπώρῳ νεκρῷ ;
sections.translation: Why do you make laws over a helpless corpse ?

sections.label: Section 1646
sections.original: Ἐτεοκλέους βουλεύματʼ , οὐχ ἡμῶν , τάδε .
sections.translation: This was Eteocles ’ purpose , not mine .

sections.label: Section 1647
sections.original: ἄφρονά γε , καὶ σὺ μῶρος ὃς ἐπίθου τάδε .
sections.translation: It is senseless , and you are a fool to obey it !

sections.label: Section 1648
sections.original: πῶς ; τἀντεταλμένʼ οὐ δίκαιον ἐκπονεῖν ;
sections.translation: How so ? Isn ’ t it right to carry out his commands ?

sections.label: Section 1649
sections.original: οὔκ , ἢν πονηρά γʼ ᾖ κακῶς τʼ εἰρημένα .
sections.translation: No ; not if they are wrong and ill-advised .

sections.label: Section 1650
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐ δικαίως ὅδε κυσὶν δοθήσεται ;
sections.translation: What ? Isn ’ t it right for that other to be given to the dogs ?

sections.label: Section 1651
sections.original: οὐκ ἔννομον γὰρ τὴν δίκην πράσσεσθέ νιν .
sections.translation: No , for the vengeance you are exacting is not a lawful one .

sections.label: Section 1652
sections.original: εἴπερ γε πόλεως ἐχθρὸς ἦν οὐκ ἐχθρὸς ὤν .
sections.translation: Yes , if he was his country ’ s enemy , when not born an enemy .

sections.label: Section 1653
sections.original: οὐκοῦν ἔδωκε τῇ τύχῃ τὸν δαίμονα .
sections.translation: Well , he rendered up his destiny to fate .

sections.label: Section 1654
sections.original: καὶ τῷ τάφῳ νυν τὴν δίκην παρασχέτω .
sections.translation: Let him now pay the penalty in his burial too .

sections.label: Section 1655
sections.original: τί πλημμελήσας , τὸ μέρος εἰ μετῆλθε γῆς ;
sections.translation: What crime did he commit , in coming to claim his portion of the land ?

sections.label: Section 1656
sections.original: ἄταφος ὅδʼ ἁνήρ , ὡς μάθῃς , γενήσεται .
sections.translation: Be very sure of this , he shall have no burial .

sections.label: Section 1657
sections.original: ἐγώ σφε θάψω , κἂν ἀπεννέπῃ πόλις .
sections.translation: I will bury him , although the state forbids .
